primary_segment: Global card payment network operating VisaNet
notes: One of two networks that clear the large majority of the world's card transactions; issuers and acquirers globally have no viable substitute for reach and interoperability.

primary_segment: Global card payment network
notes: Second half of the global card-network duopoly with Visa; together the two route the overwhelming majority of cross-border card volume.

primary_segment: Closed-loop charge/credit card network, card issuer, and merchant acquirer
notes: Closed-loop (issues its own cards and acquires its own merchants) but a distant third by volume behind Visa/Mastercard; both merchants and cardholders can route around it.

company: Discover Global Network (Capital One Financial Corp.)
primary_segment: Card network and card issuer; Discover Financial Services was acquired by Capital One (deal completed 2025-05-18)
notes: Discover Financial Services no longer trades independently. Capital One retained and continues to operate the Discover Global Network as a fourth, minority-share global card rail; not a chokepoint.

primary_segment: State-linked domestic and international bank card network and switch
notes: Mandated switch for RMB-denominated card transactions in mainland China and, by transaction value, the world's largest card network; no domestic alternative is permitted. Not listed on a public exchange; equity stakes trade only on a Chinese property/equity exchange.

primary_segment: Core banking, issuer processing, and merchant acquiring (Clover)
notes: Large but one of several competing processors (FIS, Global Payments, Fidelity, TSYS); banks and merchants can and do switch vendors.

primary_segment: Core banking software and payment processing sold to banks
notes: Competes directly with Fiserv and Jack Henry for core banking contracts; substitutable, if costly to switch.

primary_segment: Merchant acquiring and payment processing
notes: One of many merchant acquirers; competitive market with low structural lock-in at the network level.

primary_segment: Online wallet, checkout gateway, and peer-to-peer payments
notes: Dominant online-checkout brand but merchants typically offer competing wallets and card checkout alongside it.

primary_segment: Square merchant acquiring/POS hardware and Cash App peer-to-peer payments
notes: Ticker changed from SQ to XYZ effective 2025-01-21 alongside the Square-to-Block corporate rebrand. Competitive small-merchant acquiring segment.

primary_segment: Single-platform global payment processing and acquiring
notes: Enterprise merchants commonly multi-home with Stripe, Worldpay, or in-house acquiring; not structurally irreplaceable.

primary_segment: Developer-first online payment infrastructure and billing
notes: Large private fintech (last private tender valuations in the ~$90-110B range); still private with no public ticker. Merchants can and do multi-home with Adyen, Braintree, or Checkout.com.

primary_segment: Real-time payments and bank/biller payment software
notes: Software vendor to banks and networks; competes with FIS, Volante, and in-house builds.

primary_segment: European merchant acquiring and payment processing
notes: Major European acquirer but faces Adyen, Nexi, and bank-owned acquirers as substitutes.

primary_segment: Italian/European merchant acquiring and card issuing processing
notes: Regional consolidator (merged with Nets and SIA) but still one of several pan-European players.

primary_segment: Global payment gateway and acquiring platform
notes: Taken private by Advent International (with founder Philip Fayer, Novacap, and CDPQ) in a deal that completed 2024-11-15, ~US$6.3B enterprise value; no longer public, no ticker.

primary_segment: Card-issuing and processing API platform for fintechs
notes: Issuer-processing infrastructure (Cash App, Block, DoorDash cards) but competes with Galileo, i2c, and Fiserv issuer-processing lines. Completed a 1-for-4 reverse stock split effective 2026-06-30.

primary_segment: Merchant acquiring, treasury services, and wholesale payments arm of the largest US bank
notes: One of several large bank-owned acquirers/correspondent banks (also Bank of America, Citi, Wells Fargo); competitive segment despite JPM's overall balance-sheet scale.

primary_segment: Cross-border money-transfer network for consumers and businesses
notes: Moved its primary listing from London to Nasdaq on 2026-05-11 (ticker WSE), keeping a secondary LSE listing. Competes with banks' own wire/FX desks and other remittance apps.

company: SWIFT (Society for Worldwide Interbank Financial Telecommunication)
primary_segment: Member-owned cooperative operating the dominant global cross-border bank messaging network
notes: Essentially the sole global standard for interbank payment messaging. Disconnecting a country's banks from SWIFT (used against Russian and Iranian banks) functions as a de facto financial blockade, evidence that no substitute exists at scale. Member cooperative, no ticker.

company: The Clearing House Payments Company L.L.C. (CHIPS, RTP)
primary_segment: Bank-owned operator of CHIPS (large-value USD clearing) and the RTP real-time payments network
notes: Handles the bulk of large-value USD interbank clearing by dollar value, but Fedwire (central-bank operated) is a functioning alternative for final settlement, so it is not treated as a sole chokepoint here.

primary_segment: Central bank real-time gross settlement system; creates the final settlement asset for USD
notes: The literal end of the line for USD settlement: every other USD rail ultimately settles through central-bank reserve accounts, which only the Fed can create. No private alternative exists.

primary_segment: Bank-owned consortium operating the Zelle peer-to-peer payment network
notes: Convenient bank-to-bank P2P rail, but consumers can and do route around it via Venmo, Cash App, or cards.

primary_segment: Issuer of the USDC US-dollar stablecoin
notes: Completed its IPO on 2025-06-05 (NYSE: CRCL). Leading regulated US-dollar stablecoin issuer but competes with Tether and a growing field of bank and fintech stablecoins.

primary_segment: Issuer of the USDT US-dollar stablecoin, the largest stablecoin by circulation
notes: Dominant by market share and privately held (operations centered in El Salvador since a 2025-01 relicensing), but faces rising regulated competition from Circle/USDC and bank-issued stablecoins; not structurally irreplaceable.

company: NPCI (National Payments Corporation of India) / UPI
primary_segment: Nonprofit, RBI/bank-owned operator of India's UPI real-time payment switch
notes: Sole operator of UPI, the domestic switch that essentially all Indian retail digital payments route through; a government-backed monopoly with no permitted alternative rail domestically.
